reason: Load backend project rules before implementing M1 ops changes.
Keep logging, config, API, state, and Docker-related changes within the approved module boundaries.
Add SHUTTING_DOWN and probe behavior without breaking the stable {code, message, detail} service error contract.
Implement JSON logs, trace_id, build_id, kb_name, task_id, and duration fields consistently.
Apply M1 test expectations while preserving M0 behavior and avoiding M2-M4 scope creep.
Use the archived M0 contract as the baseline that M1 must not regress.
Preserve M0 AppState, storage, API, and rebuild semantics while adding ops features.
Reuse existing config/state/error patterns instead of introducing parallel ops plumbing.
Keep env config, CLI serve, Docker CMD, API lifespan, and probes aligned across layers.
